Before I generate and upload anything else, I need one thing from you because this is the point where a mistake could lock everyone out.

which looks shorter than a normal Supabase publishable key, so I don't want to assume it's complete.

You said:

Okay so I'll do nothing until you say

ChatGPT said:

Yes. Do nothing for a moment.

I want to be careful here because we're at the stage where a wrong SQL policy can either:

  • break the app completely, or

  • accidentally make parts of the database more open than intended.

So let's pause the GitHub upload.

Current status:

✅ GitHub site live
✅ Supabase project created
✅ Tables created
✅ Shane admin account created
✅ Login function created
✅ Registration function created
✅ Password hashed
✅ Supabase-connected app file prepared

Do not upload the new HTML yet.

The next thing I need to do is verify exactly which tables and columns exist in your Supabase database before I give you the final permissions SQL.

Please run this query in Supabase:

SQL
select table_name
from information_schema.tables
where table_schema = 'public'
order by table_name;

Then send me a screenshot of the results.

That lets me confirm we're building against the database you actually have, not the one I'm assuming you have. After that we'll do the final setup and then upload the new index.html.


You said:


ChatGPT said:

Perfect. That's exactly what I expected to see.

You have all 7 tables:

  • app_settings

  • app_users

  • champions

  • competitions

  • pick_history

  • player_state

  • teams

So we're working against the correct database.

Next check

Run this query:

SQL
select
  routine_name
from information_schema.routines
where routine_schema = 'public'
order by routine_name;

I want to confirm the two functions we created (app_login and app_register) are actually there before we move on to the permissions setup.

Send me the results and then we'll do the final SQL step before touching GitHub.


You said:


ChatGPT said:

Perfect.

That confirms:

✅ app_login exists
✅ app_register exists

So the database side is healthy.

Now let's do the permissions step.

Create a new SQL query and paste this entire block:

SQL
grant usage on schema public to anon;
grant usage on schema public to authenticated;

grant select, insert, update, delete on app_settings to anon;
grant select, insert, update, delete on app_users to anon;
grant select, insert, update, delete on competitions to anon;
grant select, insert, update, delete on player_state to anon;
grant select, insert, update, delete on pick_history to anon;
grant select, insert, update, delete on teams to anon;
grant select, insert, update, delete on champions to anon;

grant execute on function app_login(text,text) to anon;
grant execute on function app_register(text,text,text) to anon;

Then click Run.

Tell me whether it says:

Success

or gives an error.

After that, we'll test the database connection before uploading the new index.html.


You said:

success

ChatGPT said:

Good. Database permissions are now set.

Next step: upload the Supabase app file to GitHub.

Use this file:

Do this:

  1. Rename it on your computer to:

    index.html

  2. Go to your GitHub repo.

  3. Click the existing index.html.

  4. Click the pencil/edit icon.

  5. Delete everything in the editor.

  6. Open the downloaded index_live_supabase_v2.html in Notepad.

  7. Copy all.

  8. Paste into GitHub.

  9. Commit changes.

Then wait for GitHub Pages to redeploy.
